source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the seccomp profile is set to runtime/default in your pod definitions

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the seccomp profile is set to runtime/default in your pod definitions
input_row.description: Enable	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	in	your	pod	definitions.
input_row.rationale: Seccomp	(secure	computing	mode)	is	used	to	restrict	the	set	of	system	calls	applications can	make,	allowing	cluster	administrators	greater	control	over	the	security	of	workloads running	in	the	cluster.	Kubernetes	disables	seccomp	profiles	by	default	for	historical reasons.	You	should	enable	it	to	ensure	that	the	workloads	have	restricted	actions	available within	the	container. Impact: If	the	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	is	too	restrictive	for	you,	you	would	have	to create/manage	your	own	seccomp	profiles.	Also,	you	need	to	enable	all	alpha	features	for this	to	work.	There	is	no	individual	switch	to	turn	on	this	feature.
input_row.audit: Review	the	pod	definitions	in	your	cluster.	It	should	create	a	line	as	below: annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: runtime/default
input_row.remediation: Seccomp	is	an	alpha	feature	currently.	By	default,	all	alpha	features	are	disabled.	So,	you would	need	to	enable	alpha	features	in	the	apiserver	by	passing	"--feature- gates=AllAlpha=true"	argument. Edit	the	/etc/kubernetes/apiserver	file	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	KUBE_API_ARGS parameter	to	"--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" KUBE_API_ARGS="--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" Based	on	your	system,	restart	the	kube-apiserver	service.	For	example: 243	|	P a g e systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Use	annotations	to	enable	the	docker/default	seccomp	profile	in	your	pod	definitions.	An example	is	as	below: apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: trustworthy-pod annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: runtime/default spec: containers: - name: trustworthy-container image: sotrustworthy:latest Default	Value: By	default,	seccomp	profile	is	set	to	unconfined	which	means	that	no	seccomp	profiles	are enabled. References: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/39845 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/21790 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/seccomp.md#examples 4. https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/seccomp/ 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/clusters/seccomp/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5	Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 244	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: If	the	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	is	too	restrictive	for	you,	you	would	have	to create/manage	your	own	seccomp	profiles.	Also,	you	need	to	enable	all	alpha	features	for this	to	work.	There	is	no	individual	switch	to	turn	on	this	feature.
input_row.references: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/39845 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/21790 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/seccomp.md#examples 4. https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/seccomp/ 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/clusters/seccomp/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5	Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 244	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This control involves checking the configuration of pod definitions within a Kubernetes cluster, specifically ensuring that the seccomp profile is set to `runtime/default`. The audit process described requires reviewing the pod definitions and their annotations, which suggests a need to manually inspect YAML configuration files or use Kubernetes-native commands to verify the settings. Since this type of check involves understanding context and configuration directly related to YAML files or through APIs that expose these settings, and given that Kubernetes configurations are context-specific and may require interpreting annotations and other metadata:

- The control requires inspecting YAML/JSON configuration, which may not be fully addressable through typical Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI commands.
- The mention of reviewing pod definitions through annotations is more aligned with a manual check.
  
**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. Access the Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) cluster using the Kubectl CLI.
2. Retrieve all running pod configurations:
   ```bash
   kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o yaml
   ```
3. Manually inspect each pod definition to verify that the `seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod` annotation is set to `runtime/default`.
4. Confirm compliance by ensuring each pod has the correct seccomp profile annotation. The expected secure configuration is having `annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: runtime/default` within each pod specification.
5. If any pods do not comply, follow remediation steps to modify the pod definition and update the seccomp profile.

Given the context of the check and the nature of Kubernetes workloads, this would be best approached manually to ensure precise compliance validation.
